label: Aviludo May
rootCause: Pass C GPT copies gross_unit_price 9,99 from the Açúcar row directly below Chocolate into Chocolate's Preço Unitário field, then computes line_total_net as 2×9,99=19,98. The extracted 9,99 is exactly Açúcar's unit price — not Chocolate's printed 29,99. Dense Aviludo METRO Chef table rows are closely spaced; vertical column misalignment causes downward price bleed. Binder Rule E does not correct because the wrong triple (qty 2, price 9.99, total 19.98) is arithmetically consistent.

whyAcucarFixDidNotGeneralize.acucarFixScope: QUANTITY COLUMN ISOLATION only — prevents leading digit of 9,99 entering quantity field
whyAcucarFixDidNotGeneralize.acucarFailureMode: horizontal cross-column bleed: price digit 9 → qty field on SAME row
whyAcucarFixDidNotGeneralize.chocolateFailureMode: vertical adjacent-row bleed: entire price 9,99 → gross_unit_price on row ABOVE
whyAcucarFixDidNotGeneralize.promptGap: No rule forbids borrowing gross_unit_price from adjacent row below; Açúcar BAD example addresses qty not price; MONETARY COLUMN BINDING says 'copy digit by digit' but lacks adjacent-row isolation for Preço Unitário
whyAcucarFixDidNotGeneralize.binderGap: Rule E (neighbour bleed) requires qty×unit_price ≠ total; Chocolate wrong triple is consistent so Rule E never fires

acucar.note: Açúcar correct on v25; its 9,99 price is the bleed source for Chocolate

smallestFixCategory.recommendation: Add Chocolate BAD/GOOD example immediately after Açúcar qty rule: Chocolate 2 CX Preço 29,99 Total 59,98 — BAD if gross_unit_price reads 9,99 from Açúcar row below. Generic rule: gross_unit_price and line_total_net must come from the SAME row's columns — never from an adjacent line above or below.
notRecommended: Binder Rule E extension (consistent wrong triples slip through)

expectedFinancialImprovement.note: Chocolate is sole € contributor on May; fixing recovers full May error and ~€40 of corrected global VL (~€49 post-April-fix baseline)
